team_context.cap_situation: moderate — ~$38M in projected cap space pre-restructures, but Bosa ($41.6M) and Williams ($38.8M) carry massive cap hits. Restructures of Bosa and/or Williams could generate $17-33M additional space. Brandon Aiyuk expected to be released (post-June 1) for ~$6.3M savings.
team_context.trade_posture: Buyers at WR, DL, IOL. Core locked up long-term (Purdy, Bosa, Warner, Kittle, Lenoir, McKivitz, McCaffrey). Willing to move depth pieces for picks. Trent Williams situation could shift from extension to trade if negotiations stall.

players.name: Brock Purdy
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 2,167 yards, 20 TD, 10 INT in only 9 starts (missed 8 games with turf toe). 82.6 PFF passing grade (6th among QBs), 72.8 QBR (2nd). Led 49ers to 12-5 and playoff berth. In 2023: 4,280 yards, 31 TD, 9.6 Y/A (league-best), 73.4 QBR (1st). Franchise QB on a $265M extension with no-trade clause.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise QB under 27 on a 5-year deal with elite efficiency metrics. Untradeable due to $90.9M dead cap and full no-trade clause. Worth multiple 1sts in theory but will never be moved.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 5-year, $265M extension with a full no-trade clause. $90.9M dead cap makes a trade financially impossible. Face of the franchise.
players.injury_notes: Missed 8 games in 2025 with turf toe. Had elbow surgery after 2024 season. Durability is a mild concern — missed significant time in both 2024 and 2025.

players.name: Mac Jones
players.performance_evidence: Started 8 games for SF in 2025 while Purdy was injured, going 5-3. Earned praise from coaching staff for keeping team in playoff hunt. Competent game manager in Shanahan's system. Former first-round pick whose career arc has settled into quality backup role.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality backup QB on a cheap 1-year deal. Former 1st-round pedigree and proved he can start in a pinch. Worth a late 5th/early 6th to a team needing QB depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract, clear backup role behind Purdy. 49ers might prefer to keep him for insurance given Purdy's injury history, but would move him for a Day 3 pick.

players.name: Christian McCaffrey
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 1,202 rushing yards, 924 receiving yards — dual-threat dominance after missing most of 2024 with Achilles tendinitis. 142 yards from scrimmage and a TD vs Carolina in Week 12. 2023 NFL Offensive Player of the Year. Centerpiece of Shanahan's offense. Still the league's most versatile RB when healthy.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite production but turning 30, at a devalued position, with injury history (missed most of 2024). Cap hit is team-friendly at $9.7M in 2026. Dead cap of $22.7M makes a trade extremely unlikely. Worth a late 1st/early 2nd in a vacuum but dead cap prevents it.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $22.7M makes a trade financially devastating. Offensive engine of Shanahan's system. Still producing at elite level. No indication from team they'd consider moving him.
players.injury_notes: Missed nearly all of 2024 with bilateral Achilles tendinitis. Bounced back strong in 2025 but durability is a real concern at age 30. Has missed significant time in multiple seasons.

players.name: Jordan James
players.performance_evidence: 2025 5th-round pick from Oregon. Backup RB behind McCaffrey in Year 2. Limited rookie production as RB3/RB4 in 2025 behind McCaffrey and Guerendo. At Oregon: 1,267 rushing yards, 15 TDs in final season with 57% success rate (best in draft class). High-floor, scheme-fit back for Shanahan's system.

trade_value.reasoning: Young depth RB on a cheap rookie deal. Minimal trade value — worth a late 7th at best. More valuable to SF as McCaffrey insurance than on the trade market.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth RB on a minimum contract. 49ers would move him if a better option emerged, but wouldn't get much in return. Low priority for either side in a trade.

players.name: Mike Evans
players.performance_evidence: 2025: Only 8 games due to concussion and broken collarbone — 30 catches, 368 yards, 3 TD (career lows). Before that, 11 consecutive 1,000-yard seasons (tied NFL record with Jerry Rice). 108 career TD catches (10th all-time). Future Hall of Famer. Signed 3-year, $42.4M deal with SF in March 2026 free agency. Elite contested catch ability and red zone threat, but coming off significant injuries and turning 33.

trade_value.reasoning: Hall of Fame pedigree but age (33) and 2025 injury-shortened season cap value. Contract is structured team-friendly with potential out after Year 1. Worth a mid-4th given age, injury risk, and the fact he just signed — no team would trade for him now.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency — 49ers aren't looking to move him. But the 3-year deal likely has outs after Year 1, making him a potential trade candidate if he declines in 2026 or the team pivots. Not available right now.
players.injury_notes: Broken collarbone and concussion in 2025 limited him to 8 games. Hamstring issues earlier that season too. Draft Sharks gives 93% chance he misses at least 2 quarters in 2026. Age-related decline is the primary concern.

players.name: Ricky Pearsall
players.performance_evidence: Limited to 9 games in 2025 (hamstring, PCL injury) and had injury-shortened rookie year. Has shown flashes of quality route-running when healthy. 2026 is a make-or-break season — 49ers need to see full-season production to consider exercising 5th-year option. Still mostly projection based on draft pedigree and intermittent healthy play.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick on Year 3 of rookie deal with two years of cheap control remaining. Upside is there but injury history (17 missed games in 2 seasons) significantly discounts value. Worth a 3rd-round pick to a team betting on his health.

players.availability_reasoning: 49ers invested 1st-round capital and need WR production desperately. They want him to succeed in 2026 and won't sell low. But if he gets hurt again early, availability could shift. Not actively being shopped.
players.injury_notes: Missed 17 games in first two NFL seasons. Hamstring issues and PCL injury in 2025. Also was shot in an attempted robbery before 2024 season. Major durability concerns — 5th-year option decision looms.

players.name: Demarcus Robinson
players.performance_evidence: Veteran journeyman WR (KC, BAL, LAR, SF). Has been a reliable WR3/WR4 throughout career. Limited ceiling but dependable possession receiver. Filled in for injured teammates in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran depth WR on expiring deal. Essentially no trade value — a late 7th at most. Only useful as camp/roster depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract, aging veteran, WR room being rebuilt with Evans and potentially a draft pick. Could be a cap casualty before the season starts. Zero leverage.

players.name: George Kittle
players.performance_evidence: Torn Achilles in Wild Card playoff win vs Eagles (January 2026). Before injury: top-3 TE in the NFL for most of the past 6 years. 5x Pro Bowl, 3x All-Pro. Signed 4-year, $76.4M extension in April 2025. USAA Salute to Service Award recipient. May return by ~Week 5 of 2026 per ESPN's Wagoner. Still playing at an elite level when healthy — game-changing blocker and receiver.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite TE on a 4-year deal signed just last year, but Achilles recovery at age 32 adds uncertainty. Dead cap of $41.2M makes a trade impossible in 2026 regardless. When healthy, top-3 TE in football.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $41.2M makes this a non-starter financially. Just signed a 4-year extension. Fan favorite, locker room leader, franchise icon. Will not be traded under any circumstance.
players.injury_notes: Torn right Achilles in Wild Card win vs Eagles (January 2026). Recovery timeline suggests possible return around Week 5 of 2026. GM Lynch 'encouraged' but won't put firm timeline on it. Also missed time in 2025 with hamstring, ankle issues. At age 32, Achilles recovery is a real concern.

players.name: Jake Tonges
players.performance_evidence: Re-signed on 2-year, $8M deal in March 2026 free agency. Caught game-winning TD vs Seahawks in Week 1 2025. Useful receiving TE and capable blocker. Will be critical while Kittle recovers from Achilles. UDFA success story who has carved out a roster spot.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level TE2 who just re-signed. More valuable to SF with Kittle injured than on trade market. Worth a 6th-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed and will be the starting TE while Kittle recovers. 49ers need him badly right now. Not available unless someone overpays significantly.

players.name: Trent Williams
players.performance_evidence: PFF's 3rd-highest-graded tackle in 2025 with a 91.1 overall grade. 12x Pro Bowl, 5x First-Team All-Pro. Still performing at elite level at age 37 in 2025. Future Hall of Famer. However, entering final year of deal at age 38 with $38.8M cap hit and contentious contract negotiations.

trade_value.reasoning: Still elite but age (38) and massive cap hit ($38.8M) severely limit trade value. Dead cap of $34.1M means trading him only saves ~$4.7M. Any acquiring team would need to restructure. Worth a 2nd-round pick in talent terms but financial structure makes a trade extremely difficult.

players.availability_reasoning: Contract standoff ongoing. Schefter reports 49ers 'aren't shopping him' but Rapoport reported they're 'open to trading' if deal isn't resolved. $34.1M dead cap makes trade economics brutal — only $4.7M in savings. Extension/restructure is the most likely outcome. If released, dead cap still hits. This is more of a leverage game than actual trade availability.
players.injury_notes: Held out for part of 2024 training camp. Remarkably durable for his age — played full season in 2025 at a high level. But turning 38 before Week 1.
players.trade_buzz: Active contract standoff. Rapoport: 49ers 'open to trading' if contract not resolved. Schefter: 49ers 'aren't shopping him.' Lynch: 'confident we'll figure that out.' Williams frustrated with negotiation process. Most likely resolution is extension/restructure to lower 2026 cap hit.

players.name: Brett Toth
players.performance_evidence: Journeyman IOL brought in to compete for starting LG spot in 2026 free agency. Previous stints with Eagles and Cardinals. Left guard has been a revolving door for SF — Ben Bartch and Spencer Burford both departed in free agency. Expected to compete with Connor Colby and potentially a draft pick for the starting job.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level guard on a cheap 1-year deal. Essentially no trade value. Brought in as a camp body/potential starter.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as a free agent depth piece. If he wins the starting job he has some value, but right now he's fungible and on a minimum-level deal.

players.name: Jake Brendel
players.performance_evidence: PFF: 71.4 overall grade in 2025 (9th among NFL centers). Has anchored SF's center position since 2022 with a 70.7 PFF grade in that span. Shanahan has repeatedly praised him as one of the league's better centers. Solid in run blocking (74.6 PFF run grade), though pass protection has been a weakness (62.5 PFF pass block grade — but his best in 3 seasons).

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starting center on an affordable expiring deal. Age 32 limits long-term value. Worth a 4th/5th-round pick to a center-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract, age 32, could be replaced via draft. If 49ers draft a center or find an upgrade, Brendel becomes movable. But no obvious replacement on roster yet.

players.name: Dominick Puni
players.performance_evidence: PFF ranked 26th among NFL guards in 2025 — slight regression from impressive rookie year, partly due to early-season injury. Started all 17 games in 2024 and was entrenched as starting RG. Still only entering Year 3 on a cheap rookie deal. Ascending player with room to grow.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting guard on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years of control. IOL value is lower than premium positions, but cost-controlled starters at 25 are valuable. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, entrenched starter. 49ers would need a significant overpay (2nd-round pick) to part with him given their OL needs elsewhere.
players.injury_notes: Battled early-season injury in 2025 that may have affected play.

players.name: Colton McKivitz
players.performance_evidence: Signed 3-year, $45M extension in September 2025. PFF 34th-ranked tackle in 2024, improved further in 2025 — 85.1 pass-blocking grade in Week 1. Started 35+ consecutive games. One of team's most dependable players. Former 5th-round pick who developed into reliable RT.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting RT on a 3-year deal at $15M AAV — fair market value. Reliability and consistency are his calling cards. Dead cap around $18M limits trade viability in 2026. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd but dead cap makes it impractical.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year extension. Dead cap makes a trade impractical. Core piece of the OL. Not going anywhere.

players.name: Sebastian Valdez
players.performance_evidence: UDFA from Washington (via Montana State) who made the 53-man roster in 2025 as a rookie. Nicknamed 'The Hulk.' Showed promising pass-rush ability and strength during training camp. Listed as DE1 on current depth chart but in SF's 4-3 scheme this is more of an interior/3-tech role given his 6-3, 301 lb frame. Developmental piece who earned playing time as a rookie.

trade_value.reasoning: Unproven UDFA on a minimum deal. Some flashes as a rookie but minimal established value. Worth a 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap depth piece. Could be moved without impacting the roster significantly, but his low salary means there's no cap incentive to trade him either.

players.name: Alfred Collins
players.performance_evidence: 2025 2nd-round pick from Texas. 6-6, 332-pound interior DL. Became a starter as a rookie and was described as one of the '49ers two best defenders by end of season' alongside Upton Stout. Young building block on the defensive line. Still developing but showed significant promise in his first year.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (23) starting DT on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. High draft pedigree and encouraging rookie production. Worth a 2nd-round pick given age, contract, and upside.

players.availability_reasoning: Core young defensive piece that the 49ers drafted to rebuild their D-line. Would require a significant overpay to pry loose. Not being shopped.

players.name: Nick Bosa
players.performance_evidence: 2022 DPOY (18.5 sacks, league-best). 5x Pro Bowl, First-Team All-Pro. 64.5 career sacks. Before 2025 ACL tear: 2 sacks, 8 pressures in just 2.5 games. 142 QB hits since 2021 (most in NFL). The 49ers are 65-32 with Bosa and 6-12 without him. Recovering from torn right ACL suffered Week 3 of 2025 — his 2nd ACL tear (left knee in 2020). Expected back by training camp 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: When healthy, a top-3 edge rusher in football. But $75.8M dead cap makes a trade literally impossible. Even if he were tradeable, ACL recovery at 28 with a $41.6M cap hit would limit the market. Multiple 1sts in talent terms but will never be moved.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $75.8M — the highest on the roster — makes a trade financially absurd. Franchise-defining defender. Recovering from ACL but expected back for training camp. Lynch confirmed restructure is on the table to lower cap hit.
players.injury_notes: Torn right ACL in Week 3 of 2025 (2nd career ACL tear — left knee in 2020). Surgery in late September 2025. Lynch says he'll 'certainly' be ready by training camp. Previously returned from 2020 ACL to have DPOY-caliber seasons, but two ACL tears adds long-term durability concern.
players.trade_buzz: 49ers expected to restructure his $41.6M cap hit in 2026 to create ~$17M in space. No trade buzz whatsoever.

players.name: Dee Winters
players.performance_evidence: 6th-round pick who developed into a starting LB in 2025 after Dre Greenlaw's departure. Was second among returning LBs in snaps in 2024. Stepped into a bigger role in 2025 and held it down while Warner was injured. Solid tackling and run defense, still developing in coverage.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting LB on a dirt-cheap expiring deal. Useful player but not high-end production. Worth a 5th-round pick to a LB-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract, not a core piece with Warner entrenched as LB1. If 49ers draft a LB or add one in free agency, Winters becomes tradeable. Fair value deal would be accepted.

players.name: Fred Warner
players.performance_evidence: 5x consecutive PFF Top 101 player (No. 57 in 2025 despite injury-shortened season). All-Pro caliber LB — top-3 at the position in the NFL. Elite in coverage, tackling, and leadership. Suffered dislocated and fractured right ankle in Week 6 of 2025, ending his regular season. Lynch described his recovery as 'miraculous' and expects him back for Week 1 2026. Signed 3-year, $63M extension.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite LB on a 4-year deal with $53.7M dead cap. Untradeable financially. When healthy, arguably the best off-ball LB in football. Worth multiple 1sts in talent terms but dead cap makes it moot.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $53.7M. Defensive captain, emotional leader, best LB in football. Just signed a 3-year extension. Zero chance of being traded.
players.injury_notes: Dislocated and fractured right ankle in Week 6 of 2025 vs Buccaneers. Lynch says recovery is 'miraculous' and expects him ready for 2026. Was on verge of returning for playoffs before team was eliminated.

players.name: Renardo Green
players.performance_evidence: 2024 2nd-round pick from Florida State who stepped into a starting CB role in 2025 after Charvarius Ward's departure. Had a notable forced fumble/interception vs Seahawks in Week 1. 36 solo tackles in 2025. Still developing but showed enough to hold the CB1 spot. Missed some time with neck injury.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (25) starting CB on Year 3 of a cheap rookie deal. CB is a premium position. Not proven as elite but has 2 years of cost-controlled production ahead. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd to a CB-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, developing CB on a rookie deal. 49ers need him in their secondary after losing Ward and with limited CB depth. Would require a strong offer (2nd-round pick) to move him.
players.injury_notes: Missed time with neck injury in 2025.

players.name: Deommodore Lenoir
players.performance_evidence: Signed 5-year, ~$89M extension in November 2024. Former 5th-round pick who developed into a top-15 CB. Versatile — can play boundary and slot. Moved to boundary CB1 role in 2025 after Ward's departure. PFF's 12th-highest-paid CB reflects his standing. Strong tackler and ball-hawking ability. Key piece of rebuilt secondary.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old Pro Bowl-level CB on a 4-year deal. Premium position, ascending player, long-term control. Dead cap of ~$35M makes trade impossible in 2026. Worth a 1st-round pick in a vacuum.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a massive 5-year extension. Dead cap makes it financially impossible. Core piece of the secondary going forward. Zero chance of being moved.

players.name: Upton Stout
players.performance_evidence: 2025 3rd-round pick who earned the starting nickel CB job as a rookie. Described as one of the 49ers' 'two best defenders by end of season' alongside Alfred Collins. PFF did name him one of the NFL's worst slot corners early on, indicating a rocky start, but he clearly improved. Building block in the secondary.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (23) starting nickel CB on a cheap 3-year rookie deal. Showed real improvement as a rookie. CB is a premium position. Worth a 3rd-round pick given age and contract value.

players.availability_reasoning: Core young defensive piece. 49ers invested 3rd-round capital and see him as a building block. Not for sale unless a team grossly overpays.

players.name: Ji'Ayir Brown
players.performance_evidence: 3rd-round pick who has been a starter at safety since his rookie year. Solid tackler and competent in coverage. Not elite but dependable. Entering final year of rookie deal — extension decision coming.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting safety on an expiring cheap deal. S is not a premium position but he's a competent starter at 25. Worth a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract, not a star player. If 49ers sign or draft a safety, Brown becomes expendable. Available for fair value.

players.name: Malik Mustapha
players.performance_evidence: 2024 4th-round pick from Wake Forest. Started at FS in 2025 alongside Ji'Ayir Brown after Talanoa Hufanga's departure. Started on PUP in 2025 with knee injury but returned and took over the starting role. Physical, aggressive safety. Still developing but has 2 years of cheap control remaining.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (23) starting safety on a cheap rookie deal. Developing player at a non-premium position. Worth a 4th-round pick given age and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, and in the team's long-term plans. 49ers would need an overpay to consider moving him.
players.injury_notes: Started 2025 on PUP list with knee injury. Returned to full duties during the season.

players.name: Christian Kirk
players.performance_evidence: Kirk was released/traded by the Jaguars after their roster overhaul. In his prime Jacksonville years he posted 84/1108/8 (2022) and was a consistent chain-mover. Production dipped in 2024 amid Jacksonville's dysfunction (~60 catches, ~650 yards). Still a reliable route-runner with slot versatility who can win underneath and on intermediate concepts. Fits as a WR3 in San Francisco's YAC-heavy system. Not a high-end WR2 anymore but a quality complementary piece.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid complementary receiver on a reasonable deal with 2 years left, but turning 29 and coming off a down year caps his value. Worth roughly a late 3rd/early 4th to a team needing a proven slot option.

players.availability_reasoning: Newly acquired and slotting in as WR3 behind Deebo Samuel and Brandon Aiyuk. San Francisco brought him in for depth and competition, but he's not a core foundational piece — if a team offered a fair return, the 49ers would listen, especially given their receiver depth.

players.name: C.J. West
players.performance_evidence: West emerged as an unexpected contributor along San Francisco's defensive line in 2025 after injuries opened the door. In the 49ers' wide-9/4-3 under scheme the DE1 slot is a 5-tech/big end run defender first. West showed solid run-stopping ability and adequate pass-rush upside as a rotational piece who earned a starting role. Not a high-end starter but a capable run-defending end on a cheap deal — similar profile to Kerry Hyder or other UDFA finds the 49ers have developed on the DL.

trade_value.reasoning: A below-average starter on a minimum-level deal has some value as a cheap contributor, but UDFA pedigree and lack of proven high-end production cap his trade ceiling at a 5th/6th-round pick equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Functional starter but not someone the 49ers would be unwilling to move if they received a mid-to-late round pick. Defensive line depth is an organizational strength and they could replace his production internally.

players.name: Dre Greenlaw
players.performance_evidence: Greenlaw was a quality starter and key piece of SF's defense before tearing his Achilles in Super Bowl LVIII (Feb 2024). He missed the entire 2024 season recovering. Reports suggest he returned to team activities in 2025 but was working back from the devastating injury. Pre-injury he was a solid all-around linebacker — good in coverage for the position, physical downhill run defender, and an emotional leader. Post-Achilles, the big question is how much burst and lateral agility he's recovered. Even if he's 85-90% of his former self, he's a capable starter but no longer the above-average player he was in 2022-2023.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring contract ($0 dead cap) with major Achilles injury history and diminished physical profile post-recovery. A team needing LB help might offer a 5th-rounder, but the injury risk and impending free agency significantly limit value.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract with zero dead cap makes him a clean trade piece. The 49ers have invested in younger linebacker options and Greenlaw's post-Achilles status makes him a candidate to be moved before the deadline or at the draft if SF can recoup any pick value. Beat writers have noted the team is managing his snap count carefully, suggesting they may not view him as a long-term piece.
players.injury_notes: Torn Achilles in Super Bowl LVIII (February 2024). Missed entire 2024 season. Long-term concerns about lateral agility and explosiveness post-recovery at age 28.
players.trade_buzz: No specific reporting of active trade discussions, but his expiring deal and injury history make him a logical trade candidate for a contender needing mid-season LB depth.

players.name: Mykel Williams
players.performance_evidence: Williams was selected 11th overall in the 2025 NFL Draft out of Georgia, where he was a dominant edge defender with elite physical tools (6'5", 265 lbs, explosive first step). At Georgia he tallied 8.5 sacks and 13 TFLs in 2024 as a consensus first-round talent. In the 49ers' scheme, the LB4 slot corresponds to the SAM/strong-side OLB who rushes the passer — an EDGE role. As a rookie entering year 2, he projects as a quality starter with upside to become a Pro Bowl-caliber edge rusher. First-round rookies at EDGE typically take 1-2 years to hit their stride, but Williams' physical profile and Georgia pedigree suggest a high floor.

trade_value.reasoning: First-round EDGE rusher on a full 5-year rookie deal with elite physical tools and ascending trajectory. Young premium-position players on cheap long-term contracts are the most valuable trade assets in football. Worth a mid-to-late 1st-round pick — comparable to what teams have paid for young EDGE talent historically.

players.availability_reasoning: A 22-year-old former top-12 pick at the most premium non-QB position, still on his full rookie contract with 5 years of team control including a 5th-year option. The 49ers drafted him to be a cornerstone of their defense for the next half-decade. The dead cap alone ($27M) makes a trade financially devastating, and the positional value and youth make him irreplaceable. Absolutely no chance SF moves him.
